title: Personal Services Income received 30 June PM
body:
O.P.
I now work part-time for one client on contract through my personal company and need to treat that income as personal services income. At the end of the financial year the client sometimes (not always) clears their backlog of invoices payable and pays late PM on 30th June, which they did yesterday.
My super fund and payroll app requires all to be complete about 20 June, which I do. I can't overpay myself in anticipation of an invoice being paid but this year my company received income that it could not possibly pay me. GST, wages and company PAYG are paid quarterly. My wages are paid quarterly depending on income, sometimes is nil.
The company is small scale, and I do not use an accountant, but I do use an accountant to lodge my personal tax return. All wages are now done using Payroller and a super clearing house, so myGov prefill already includes the actual wages made.
Option 1 is to record the payment in the accounting package (Gnucash) on1st July 2027 and handle it as being received in FY2027 and then pay wages as per normal.
Option 2 is to include the income in FY2026 but then the company retains profits, affecting the balance sheet, and I would need to get the income as a dividend with franking credits.
Neither seems right but I suspect this is fairly common occurrence. Is there a recommended way to handle this? I prefer Option 1.
